title: Huge Lava Tube Discovered on Venus!
summary:
Scientists have discovered a giant underground cave on Venus! It's a lava tube—a tunnel that forms when molten lava flows and then drains away. This one is huge: at least 375 meters deep and maybe up to 1 kilometer wide. That's bigger than any lava tube on Earth or Mars!

The discovery came from old radar images taken by NASA's Magellan probe in the 1990s. Scientists used new techniques to reanalyze the data and spotted a collapsed "skylight"—a hole where the roof of the lava tube had caved in. It's near a volcano called Nyx Mons.

This is the first lava tube found on Venus. Finding it will help future missions like NASA's VERITAS and Europe's EnVision, which will study Venus with better radar.

But don't expect astronauts to visit this cave anytime soon. Venus is super hot—hot enough to melt electronics—and the pressure at the surface is 93 times greater than on Earth. So this lava tube will stay a secret beneath the clouds.
why_it_matters: This shows that even faraway places like Venus can hold secrets that help us understand how planets work—just like learning about different environments on Earth.

keywords.explanation: A tunnel underground that forms when hot lava flows and then empties, leaving an empty cave.
A planet next to Earth that is very hot and covered with thick clouds.
A big hole or tunnel underground, like a natural room.
A tool that uses radio waves to see things that are far away or hidden.
A spacecraft without people that explores planets and sends data back to Earth.
The name of a NASA spacecraft that mapped Venus using radar in the 1990s.

questions.question: Where was the giant lava tube found?
How did scientists discover the lava tube?
options: By sending a robot to Venus
By looking at old radar images from the Magellan probe
By digging a hole on Earth
By using a telescope in space
questions.correct_answer: By looking at old radar images from the Magellan probe

questions.question: What is the name of the volcano near the lava tube?

options: At least 375 meters deep and maybe 1 kilometer wide
It is only as big as a car
questions.correct_answer: At least 375 meters deep and maybe 1 kilometer wide

options: Venus is too hot and has crushing pressure
There is no air on Venus
questions.correct_answer: Venus is too hot and has crushing pressure

questions.question: Which future missions will study Venus with better radar?

background_read: Venus is Earth's closest planetary neighbor, but it is extremely hot—around 465°C (870°F)—and has a thick, poisonous atmosphere.
Lava tubes are also found on Earth and on the Moon, but this one on Venus is the biggest known.
The Magellan probe (a NASA spacecraft that orbited Venus in the 1990s) used radar to map the planet's surface through its thick clouds.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists reanalyzing old radar images
WHAT: Discovered a giant underground lava tube on Venus, the first ever found there
WHERE: Venus, near a volcano called Nyx Mons
WHY: The discovery will help future missions (VERITAS and EnVision) study Venus with better radar and learn more about volcanic activity on the planet

perspectives.description: Scientists are excited because this is the first lava tube found on Venus, and it gives clues about the planet's volcanic past.
Venus is so hot and the pressure is so great that no spacecraft or astronaut can go inside the tube soon, so it remains a mystery for now.
Finding this tube will help plan future missions like VERITAS and EnVision, which will use better radar to see even more details of Venus's surface.
